next_head_excerpt:
# Chapter 13

The pub was a Muggle place near Charing Cross Road, a narrow room with uncomfortable chairs and middling beer and no associations. She and Ron had started using it after the divorce because neither of them had ever kissed or argued or decided anything in this building, which was exactly why it worked. No memory to navigate around. Just a room.

She'd Apparated into the alley behind the used bookshop three doors down and walked the rest, her coat pulled close against the December cold. The Floo would have been easier but the Floo was unreliable now — three failures in the last week alone, connections dropping mid-transit, and she didn't fancy rematerializing in someone's kitchen in Birmingham because the network had lost her routing. So Apparition, with its familiar squeeze and the ringing in her ears afterward, and then ordinary London pavement under her boots, and the simple solidity of it — pavement that stayed where it was, walls that didn't rearrange themselves when she wasn't looking — felt strange. Not bad. Just strange, the way any normal thing feels after weeks inside the abnormal.

Ron was already there. Always early now, the way he'd never been when they were married. He stood when he saw her, which was new too — some politeness he'd adopted after the separation that she hadn't asked for and didn't correct because correcting it would mean discussing it, and discussing it would mean admitting the shape of their interactions had changed.

"Hey." He kissed her cheek. His face was cold. "You look — you look good."

It came out slightly wrong, the complim...
open_hooks_to_carry: Draco's confusion and hurt at her withdrawal — he interprets it as rejection and retreats into his own distance
The Manor responds to their mutual withdrawal: corridors lengthen, archive won't open, the house goes cold
The scar reveal: glamour flickers or sleeve rides up during examination. MUDBLOOD carved in her forearm. He touches it. She flinches. His hand stays
The scene that follows: the most intimate so far. No kiss. He traces the letters. The silence. His honesty and her question

payoffs_to_land.description: The scar she has been hiding since Ch1 is seen by the person whose house it was carved in. The concealment habit established across multiple chapters makes the exposure land with full weight

prev_tail_excerpt:
...use had been designed so she couldn't tell. And the recursion — I can't tell whether my feelings are real, and I can't tell whether my inability to tell is a product of the architecture — fit so precisely into the shape of her own specific weakness that it felt almost personal. She'd spent her entire life needing to understand before she acted. Needing to verify before she committed. Needing certainty before she let herself feel anything she couldn't take back. And the house had built a trap that was her exact shape: a doubt that could never be fully resolved because the instrument of resolution was itself compromised.

She pulled the blanket over her legs without undressing. Above her, through the resonance, Draco's awareness had gone quiet — the slow, diffuse texture of sleep. He was dreaming, or close to it. She could feel the edges of his unconscious attention the way you could feel the heat of a banked fire from across a room — faint, ambient, impossible to ignore once you knew it was there.

Tomorrow she would wake up and examine his Mark. She would eat his sandwiches. She would sit across from him in the library and feel his presence through the resonance and she would do all of it under knowledge she was not sharing. And the word for that was not verification. The word for that was not prudence or due diligence or responsible scholarship.

She knew the word. She was precise enough, and honest enough with herself if with no one else, to name it.

The house was warm around her. The house was always warm. She lay in the dark and let the lie stand and she did not sleep.
state_deltas_required: Hermione's withdrawal is incomprehensible to Draco; the scar reveal during a Mark examination strips the last layer of concealment between them
He has seen the full cost of what happened in his house. The power dynamic shifts — she is no longer the competent researcher helping the damaged heir. They are both marked
